text: Does the Gaussian wave packet v0=4 exp(-(x+5)^2/2.25), u0=0 decompose into a soliton train (>=2 well-separated peaks, each amp>=0.8, mass drift<8%) under the coupled Burgers-swept-KdV system by T=6.0?

method: Fourier-pseudospectral spatial derivatives (no dealiasing) + fully explicit classical RK4 on every term
bank_use_rationale: Progressive-complexity discipline forbids adopting IMEX-CN at E1. kb-kdv-IMEX-CN-spectral-pass confirms Fourier pseudospectral is the right SPATIAL family for KdV-like dispersion on Nx=256 / [-15,15]; we keep that spatial choice but use explicit RK4 (the simplest meaningful time integrator) so failures can be localized to the time-stepping/dealiasing axis. No negative bank is available in this condition.

runtime_warnings: subsequent NaN in u*v, gx, hx

rationale: Explicit RK4 on Fourier-pseudospectral with NO dealiasing blows up at t=0.42 (~7% of the way to T=6). The first overflow appears in v*v, the classic signature of aliasing-driven nonlinear instability — high-k modes generated by the v^2 product fold back onto the resolved spectrum, grow exponentially under the pure-imaginary v_xxx eigenvalues (RK4 has only marginal damping there), and overflow. Diagnostic conclusion: aliasing is the dominant failure mode, not raw dispersive CFL. Single-component upgrade: add 2/3 dealiasing.

Apply ONE single-component upgrade over E1: add 2/3-rule dealiasing on every nonlinear product (v^2, u*v, v*v_x, u*u_x). Keep explicit RK4 time integrator and Fourier-pseudospectral derivatives unchanged so the contrast with E1 isolates aliasing as the failure cause. This matches the bank's general spectral-soliton recipe (kb-gardner-G2-IMEX-CN-dealiased-stableRadiation, kb-kdv-spectral-solitonAmplitude-conservation) on the dealiasing axis without yet adopting the IMEX time integrator.

method: Fourier-pseudospectral derivatives + explicit RK4 + 2/3-rule dealiasing on every nonlinear product (v*v, u*v, v*v_x, u*u_x)
bank_use_rationale: Bank entries kb-gardner-G2-IMEX-CN-dealiased-stableRadiation and kb-kdv-spectral-solitonAmplitude-conservation both use 2/3-rule dealiasing on spectral KdV/Gardner problems. We adopt that single component (dealiasing) without yet promoting the time integrator to IMEX-CN — discipline allows only one change per step. If E2 still fails due to the dispersive stiffness with explicit RK4 (not aliasing), the bank's recommended next step is IMEX-CN, which would become E3.

rationale: Phenomenon target met with a wide margin. The Gaussian wave packet decomposed into many well-separated peaks: 14 peaks with amplitude >= 0.8 (target asks for >= 2). Peak spacings range from 1.3 to 3.6 grid units, all >> grid dx=0.117 — peaks are real, physically separated structures (not grid-scale oscillations). Mass(v) drift = -0.6% (target < 8%). Same peak count survives a hard low-pass at |k| <= 6 (14 peaks >= 0.8) confirming peaks live at moderate wavenumbers, not grid noise. The result is consistent with the integrable / near-integrable Burgers-swept-KdV/Gardner reduction's inverse-scattering soliton-train decomposition of a tall Gaussian. The growth of max|v| from 2 (mid-run) to 4.4 (final) is the Burgers-swept coupling feeding energy from u back into v after u itself was generated by the source 3*v^2 — this is physical coupled-system behaviour, not blow-up (mass is conserved to 0.6%).

E2 already exceeds the phenomenon target by a wide margin (14 peaks >= 0.8 vs required >=2; mass drift -0.6% vs allowed <8%) and the solution is all-finite. Bank entry kb-gardner-KdV-method-transfer-moderate-amplitude explicitly limits positive IMEX-CN transfer to amplitudes in [1, 2]; our amp=4 IC falls outside that range, so promoting to IMEX-CN at E3 carries a non-trivial blow-up risk and would not be a strict improvement. Save E2 result as final answer.
